questions: How does the RAG pipeline work?
How do I deploy this system?
contexts: This is a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) chatbot system designed for AI SaaS applications. It combines document retrieval with large language models to provide accurate, context-grounded answers.
The system uses Google Gemini and Groq LLMs with ChromaDB for vector storage.
The RAG pipeline retrieves relevant documents from the vector database, then passes them as context to the LLM along with the user's question.
The LLM generates an answer grounded in the retrieved context, ensuring factual accuracy.
Supported formats include PDF, TXT, CSV, Excel (.xlsx, .xls), and JSON files.
The ingestion pipeline automatically detects file types and loads them appropriately.
ChromaDB uses embeddings from BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 to convert text into vector representations.
Similarity search finds the most relevant documents based on cosine distance in embedding space.
The system supports multiple LLM providers: Groq (ultra-fast, free tier available) and Google Gemini.
Providers can be switched via the PROVIDER environment variable.
Chat history is persisted in a SQLite database (chat_memory.db).
Each conversation is stored with session IDs, allowing multi-turn context awareness.
Ragas is used for evaluation, providing metrics like faithfulness, relevancy, and precision.
Golden datasets enable quantitative assessment of RAG quality.
Docker Compose orchestrates both the API and ChromaDB services.
The system can be deployed on Kubernetes or any cloud platform supporting containers.
ground_truths: This is a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system for AI SaaS applications that combines document retrieval with LLMs to provide accurate, context-grounded answers.
The RAG pipeline retrieves relevant documents from a vector database, passes them as context to an LLM, and generates answers grounded in the retrieved context.
The system supports PDF, TXT, CSV, Excel, and JSON file formats for ingestion.
ChromaDB uses sentence embeddings (BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5) to represent text as vectors, then performs similarity search using cosine distance.
The system supports Groq (ultra-fast) and Google Gemini as LLM providers, switchable via the PROVIDER environment variable.
Chat history is persisted in SQLite (chat_memory.db) with session-based storage for multi-turn conversations.
Ragas framework is used for evaluation with metrics including faithfulness, relevancy, and precision based on golden datasets.
The system is containerized with Docker Compose for easy deployment across development, staging, and production environments.
